LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. PURSUANT TO THE PARTIES' STIPULATION, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS HEATHER GARRITY, ETHAN GARRITY AND DOMINIC GARRITY'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE NOTICE OF MOTION ONLY STATES THAT DEFENDANTS ARE SEEKING A CHANGE OF VENUE BASED ON FORUM NON CONVENIENS GROUNDS. (CCP SEC. 1010, "THE NOTICE OF MOTION MUST STATE'THE GROUNDS UPON WHICH IT WILL BE MADE.) "ON A MOTION FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS, DEFENDANT, AS THE MOVING PARTY, BEARS THE BURDEN OF PROOF." (STANGVIK V. SHILEY INC. (1991) 54 CAL.3D 744, 751.) DEFENDANTS HAVE OFFERED NO EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING WHAT WITNESSES WILL BE TESTIFYING, EXPLAINING HOW A CHANGE OF VENUE WOULD BE MORE CONVENIENT FOR THEM, OR SHOWING HOW THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WOULD BE PROMOTED BY A CHANGE OF VENUE. (SEE HARDEN V. SKINNER AND HAMMOND (1955) 130 CAL.APP.2D 750, 754.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS HEATHER GARRITY, ETHAN GARRITY, AND DOMINIC GARRITY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE STALKING CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE PAR. 22 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE INTRUSION CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE PAR. 21 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE FALSE LIGHT CAUSE OF ACTION AND THE CLAIM STANDS OR FALLS WITH THE DEFAMATION CLAIM. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE HARASSMENT CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE PARS. 17 AND 18 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE DEFAMATION CAUSE OF ACTION AND IT IS NOT TIME BARRED. (SEE PAR. 19 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE PARS. 58 AND 59 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE INTERFERENCE CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE PARS. 61-65 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT). THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE NEGLIGENT SUPERVISION CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE PARS. 66-70 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT). THE COURT AGREES THAT CONSPIRACY IS NOT A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT WILL TREAT THE "NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION" FOR CONSPIRACY AS GENERAL CHARGING ALLEGATIONS. THE CONSPIRACY THEORY IS VIABLE BECAUSE PLAINTIFF PLEADS AN UNDERLYING WRONG AND AN AGREEMENT. TO ATTACK THE RECOVERY OF DAMAGES, DEFENDANTS SHOULD HAVE FILED A MOTION TO STRIKE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANTS HEATHER GARRITY, ETHAN GARRITY, AND DOMINIC GARRITY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT DO NOT IMPLICATE A PUBLIC ISSUE UNDER CCP 425.16(E)(4). SEE RIVERO V. AMERICAN FEDERATION OF STATE, COUNTY AND MUNICIPAL EMPLOYEES, AFL-CIO (2003) 105 CAL.APP.4TH 913, 925. DEFENDANT FAILS TO MEET ITS BURDEN ON THE FIRST PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. ORDER SIGNED. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert M. Mitchell, Esq. of Diamond Baker Mitchell LLP, Tel. 530.272.9977.x204, for defendants and moving party;
Jeremy L. Tissot, Esq. of The Tissot Law Firm, Tel. 310.274.8525, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS HEATHER GARRITY, ETHAN GARRITY, and DOMINIC GARRITY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The anti-SLAPP motion to strike is denied. The allegations of the amended complaint do not implicate a public issue under CCP 425.16(e)(4). See Rivero v. American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees, AFL-CIO (2003) 105 Cal.App.4th 913, 925. Defendant fails to meet its burden on the first prong of the anti-SLAPP statute. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert M. Mitchell, Esq. of Diamond Baker Mitchell LLP, Tel. 530.272.9977.x204, for defendants and moving party;
Jeremy L. Tissot, Esq. of The Tissot Law Firm, Tel. 310.274.8525, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS HEATHER GARRITY, ETHAN GARRITY, and DOMINIC GARRITY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in its entirety. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the stalking cause of action. (See par. 22 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the intrusion cause of action. (See par. 21 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the false light cause of action and the claim stands or falls with the defamation claim. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the harassment cause of action. (See pars. 17 and 18 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the defamation cause of action and it is not time barred. (See par. 19 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. (See pars. 58 and 59 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the interference cause of action. (See pars. 61-65 of the amended complaint). The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the negligent supervision cause of action. (See pars. 66-70 of the amended complaint). The Court agrees that conspiracy is not a distinct cause of action. The Court will treat the "ninth cause of action" for conspiracy as general charging allegations. The conspiracy theory is viable because plaintiff pleads an underlying wrong and an agreement. To attack the recovery of damages, defendants should have filed a motion to strike. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Craig A. Diamond, Esq. of Diamond Baker LLP, Tel. 530.272.9977 x203, for defendants and moving party via Court Call;
Jeremy L. Tissot, Esq. of The Tissot Law Firm, Tel. 310.274.8525, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was not argued. Pursuant to the parties' stipulation, the Court modified its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs HEATHER GARRITY, ETHAN GARRITY and DOMINIC GARRITY's MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is denied without prejudice. The Notice of Motion only states that Defendants are seeking a change of venue based on forum non conveniens grounds. (CCP sec. 1010, "The notice of motion must state'the grounds upon which it will be made.) "On a motion for forum non conveniens, defendant, as the moving party, bears the burden of proof." (Stangvik v. Shiley Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 751.) Defendants have offered no evidence establishing what witnesses will be testifying, explaining how a change of venue would be more convenient for them, or showing how the ends of justice would be promoted by a change of venue. (See Harden v. Skinner and Hammond (1955) 130 Cal.App.2d 750, 754.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
